per_question.question: What's the difference between the Decreasing Cover and Level Cover options for the Death Benefit?
per_question.reference_answer: Under Option A (Decreasing Cover), the Sum Assured decreases over the Period of Coverage in line with the loan repayment schedule as specified in the Certificate of Insurance. Under Option B (Level Cover), the Sum Assured remains constant throughout the Period of Coverage as chosen by the Master Policyholder or Member and specified in the Certificate of Insurance. The moratorium option is only available with Option A.
per_question.generated_answer:
The difference between the Decreasing Cover and Level Cover options for the Death Benefit is as follows:

1. **Decreasing Cover (Option A)**: If this option is chosen, the Sum Assured payable upon the death of the Member will be the Sum Assured outstanding as per the schedule indicated in the Certificate of Insurance, regardless of the actual loan outstanding at the time of death [Source: Page 9].

2. **Level Cover (Option B)**: If this option is chosen, the Sum Assured payable will be the amount specified in the Certificate of Insurance, which remains constant throughout the coverage period [Source: Page 10]. 

In summary, the Decreasing Cover option results in a Sum Assured that decreases over time, while the Level Cover option maintains a constant Sum Assured.

per_question.reference_answer: The member is eligible for coverage as they are between 15 and 69 years of age at entry. 9.7.1. The person named by the Member as the Nominee in the enrollment form for Insurance and whose 
name has been registered and recorded by the Master Policyholder in the Register of Members in 
accordance with Section 39 of the Insurance Act, shall be the person to whom the Death Benefit under 
this Policy will be paid by Us in the event of death of the Member.  
9.7.2. A change in nomination may be made by the Member at any time during the Period of Coverage by 
informing the Master Policyholder in writing and registering the same with the Master Policyholder.  
9.7.3. Where the Nominee is a minor, the Member is required to appoint an appointee to receive the Death 
Benefit in the event of the Member’s death during the minority of the Nominee.

appointee with the Nominee, to receive the Death Benefit on behalf of the minor Nominee.

per_question.question: I'm 68 and applying for a loan. Am I eligible for coverage?
per_question.reference_answer: Yes, you are eligible if you are a natural person between 15 and 69 years of age on the Date of Commencement of Risk or Entry Date, and will not be more than 70 years of age on the date the Insurance expires. The proposed Period of Coverage must be at least 1 year.

9.5.1. Declaration of the correct Age of the Member(s) is important for Our underwriting process, before 
issuance of this Policy and/or Certificate of Insurance(s). The Premiums are calculated on the basis of 
the Age of the Member(s). If the Age declared in the Proposal Form and/or Member enrollement 
application form is found to be incorrect anytime during the Policy Term and/or Period of Coverage at 
the time of claim, then subject to Section 9.6, We may at Our discretion:

cancel the Policy and/or the Certificate of Insurance and pay the Surrender Value; or

adjust the Premium payable by the Master Policyholder/ the Member or Death Benefit payable to

per_question.question: If a member paid a premium of ₹10,000 and surrenders after 24 months of a 60-month coverage period with Level Cover Sum Assured of ₹5,00,000, what is the surrender value?
per_question.reference_answer: Using the surrender value formula: Surrender Value = 70% of Premium paid x (Unexpired Period of Coverage in months / Total Period of Coverage in months) x (Sum Assured on surrender date / Sum Assured on commencement). Unexpired period = 60 - 24 = 36 months. For Level Cover, the Sum Assured ratio is 1. So: 0.70 x ₹10,000 x (36/60) x 1 = ₹4,200.
per_question.generated_answer:
To calculate the surrender value, we can use the formula provided in the context:

Surrender Value = 70% of Premium paid * (Unexpired Period of Coverage in months on the date of Surrender / Total Period of Coverage in months) * (Sum Assured applicable on the date of Surrender / Sum Assured on the Date of Commencement of Risk / Effective Date of Coverage)

Given:
- Premium paid = ₹10,000
- Unexpired Period of Coverage = 60 months - 24 months = 36 months
- Total Period of Coverage = 60 months
- Sum Assured applicable on the date of Surrender = ₹5,00,000
- Sum Assured on the Date of Commencement of Risk = ₹5,00,000

Now, substituting the values into the formula:

1. Calculate 70% of Premium paid:
   70% of ₹10,000 = ₹7,000

2. Calculate the fraction of the unexpired period:
   Unexpired Period of Coverage in months / Total Period of Coverage in months = 36 / 60 = 0.6

3. Calculate the fraction of the sum assured:
   Sum Assured applicable on the date of Surrender / Sum Assured on the Date of Commencement of Risk = ₹5,00,000 / ₹5,00,000 = 1

Now, putting it all together:

Surrender Value = ₹7,000 * 0.6 * 1 = ₹4,200

Therefore, the surrender value is ₹4,200.

per_question.question: My spouse passed away — what documents do I need to submit a claim?
per_question.reference_answer: You need to submit: (i) claimant's statement in the prescribed form; (ii) original Certificate of Insurance; (iii) original death certificate issued by the local or municipal authority; (iv) identity proof of the Member and the Nominee(s) bearing their photographs and signatures; and (v) any other documents or information required by the Company for assessing and approving the claim request.

9.3.1. Notwithstanding anything stated herein, if a Member commits suicide, whether sane or insane, within 1 
(One) year from the Date of Commencement of Risk /Effective Date of Coverage or Entry Date, as the 
case may be, such Member’s Insurance shall cease immediately and no Death Benefit is payable under 
this Policy in relation to such Member.

per_question.question: How soon must I notify Max Life after a death?
per_question.reference_answer: The Master Policyholder must notify Max Life Insurance in writing of the happening of the Insured Event within 30 days from the date of the event. The claim should be registered at the Claims Unit, Max Life Insurance Company Limited, Plot 90A, Sector 18, HUDA, Gurugram 122002, Haryana, India.

per_question.question: Can I cancel my coverage and get money back?
per_question.reference_answer: Yes, a Member may at any time request surrender of his Insurance by making a written request through the Master Policyholder or directly to the Company. The Surrender Value is calculated as: 70% of Premium paid multiplied by (Unexpired Period of Coverage in months / Total Period of Coverage in months) multiplied by (Sum Assured applicable on the date of Surrender / Sum Assured on the Date of Commencement of Risk).
per_question.generated_answer: Yes, you can cancel your coverage and get money back. You have a period of 15 days (or 30 days if the policy was issued through distance marketing) to review the terms and conditions of the policy. If you are not satisfied, you can return the original policy and state your objections in writing. Upon cancellation, the policy will terminate, and you will receive a refund of the premiums paid, after deducting certain expenses, but the refund will not be less than 80% of the premiums received [Source: Page 1][Source: Page 13].

However, the refund of the Premium by Us in no case shall be less than 80% (Eighty Percent) of the 
Premium received by Us in respect of such Member. 
9.3.2. For the sake of clarity, if a Borrower commits suicide, whether sane or insane, within 1 (One) year from 
the Date of Commencement of Risk /Effective Date of Coverage or Entry Date, as the case may be, 
such Borrower’s Insurance shall cease immediately and no Death Benefit is payable under this Policy in 
relation to such Borrower.

per_question.question: Under what circumstances can Max Life Insurance repudiate or cancel a claim or policy?
per_question.reference_answer: The Company can repudiate a claim or cancel the policy in several circumstances: (1) if the age declared is found to be incorrect, the Company may cancel and pay Surrender Value or adjust benefits (Section 9.5); (2) concealment, non-disclosure, misrepresentation, or fraud renders the policy voidable, and the Company may cancel and pay Surrender Value once fraud is established under Section 45 of the Insurance Act (Section 9.6.2); (3) if policy conditions are contravened or untrue averments are made, the Insurance ceases and the policy may be cancelled (Section 9.6.3); (4) if a fraudulent claim is made, the policy becomes void (Section 9.10); (5) the Company may also scrutinize documents and investigate the cause of death to repudiate partially or completely (Section 5.3).
per_question.generated_answer:
Max Life Insurance can repudiate or cancel a claim or policy under the following circumstances:

1. **Concealment, Non-disclosure, Misrepresentation, or Fraud**: Any of these actions shall render the policy and/or the certificate of insurance liable for cancellation and/or voidable at their option [Source: Page 14].

2. **Contravention of Conditions**: If any condition contained in the policy or any endorsement is contravened, or if it appears that an untrue or incorrect statement is made in the proposal form, enrollment form, or other documents, the death benefit under the policy may be void, and the insurance may cease [Source: Page 14].

3. **Fraud or Misrepresentation Established**: If fraud or misrepresentation is established by Max Life Insurance, they may cancel the policy and pay the surrender value, in accordance with Section 45 of the Insurance Act [Source: Page 14].

These conditions highlight the importance of full disclosure and the consequences of failing to provide accurate information.

reserve the right to cancel the Policy and pay the Surrender Value subject to the fraud or 
misrepresentation being established by Us, in accordance with Section 45 of the Insurance Act.  
9.6.3. If any condition herein contained or endorsement made hereto is contravened or if it appears that an 
untrue or incorrect averment is contained in the Proposal Form, enrollment form, documents or other 
statements furnished to Us by the Master Policyholder or any Member or that any material information 
has been withheld, then, subject to Section 45 of the Insurance Act, 1938, the Death Benefit under this 
Policy in so far as the same relate to a Member shall be void and the relative Insurance shall cease and 
be determined.

Premiums received by Us, after deducting the proportionate risk Premium for the period of cover,

9.6. Full Disclosure and Incontestability  
9.6.1. Insurance is a contract of utmost good faith and We rely and trust upon the Master Policyholder’s and 
the Member(s) representations. This Policy has been underwritten and issued by Us on the Master 
Policyholder’s representation that the Master Policyholder and the Member/s has/ have made and/ or 
caused to be made full disclosure of all relevant facts and circumstances. We shall not be liable for any 
action taken in good faith upon any data or document or information or evidence so furnished which 
shall be or shall prove to have been incorrect or erroneous or inaccurate.  
9.6.2. Any concealment, non-disclosure, misrepresentation, or fraud shall render the Policy and/or the 
Certificate of Insurance liable for cancellation and/ or voidable at Our option.

per_question.question: What happens if the group policy is cancelled?
per_question.reference_answer: Either the Master Policyholder or the Company may terminate this Policy by giving 90 days prior written notice to the other party. Upon termination, each Member's coverage continues as an individual policy until the date of expiration of the Period of Coverage. No new enrollment forms will be accepted from the date of termination.
per_question.generated_answer:
If the group policy is cancelled, the following occurs:

1. The policy may be terminated by either the Master Policyholder or the Company with a 90 days prior written notice. In this case, each Member’s coverage under the Insurance will continue as an individual policy until the expiration of the Period of Coverage [Source: Page 16].

2. No new enrollment forms for Eligible Members will be accepted from the date of termination, but all obligations regarding the Members enrolled under the policy will continue until the expiry of their Period of Coverage or surrender of the Certificate of Insurance, whichever is earlier [Source: Page 16].

3. If the policy is returned by the Master Policyholder or the Certificate of Insurance is returned by the Member, the policy or insurance will terminate immediately, and all rights, benefits, and interests will cease. A refund of the premiums received will be provided after deducting certain charges [Source: Page 13].

In summary, cancellation leads to the termination of the group policy, but existing Members retain their coverage until the end of their individual policies.

per_question.question: Do I need a medical exam to get coverage?
per_question.reference_answer: Not necessarily. The Free Cover Limit is the Sum Assured amount up to which Insurance cover can be allowed based on simple insurability conditions without requiring any evidence of health. If the Sum Assured exceeds the Free Cover Limit, health evidence may be required.
per_question.generated_answer: Yes, a medical examination may be required for all Eligible Members whose total proposed Sum Assured, combined with amounts already insured or proposed to be insured under other policies, exceeds the Free Cover Limit. This is determined by the insurer as specified in the context [Source: Page 9].

9.7. Nomination  
9.7.1. The person named by the Member as the Nominee in the enrollment form for Insurance and whose 
name has been registered and recorded by the Master Policyholder in the Register of Members in 
accordance with Section 39 of the Insurance Act, shall be the person to whom the Death Benefit under 
this Policy will be paid by Us in the event of death of the Member.  
9.7.2. A change in nomination may be made by the Member at any time during the Period of Coverage by 
informing the Master Policyholder in writing and registering the same with the Master Policyholder.  
9.7.3. Where the Nominee is a minor, the Member is required to appoint an appointee to receive the Death 
Benefit in the event of the Member’s death during the minority of the Nominee.

9.2.3. The Master Policyholder shall furnish to Us all information, documentation and evidence which We 
may require with regard to any matter pertaining to this Policy. All documents furnished to the Master 
Policyholder by any Member in connection with the Insurance and other records which may have a 
bearing on the Insurance under this Policy, shall be informed to Us and shall be open for Our inspection 
at all reasonable times.  
9.3. Suicide Exclusion  
9.3.1. Notwithstanding anything stated herein, if a Member commits suicide, whether sane or insane, within 1 
(One) year from the Date of Commencement of Risk /Effective Date of Coverage or Entry Date, as the 
case may be, such Member’s Insurance shall cease immediately and no Death Benefit is payable under 
this Policy in relation to such Member.
